expectations.text: Places Stripe access in an adapter (src/adapters/) wired via Container, not in the service
expectations.evidence: Puts Stripe in modules/billing/stripe.gateway.ts (inside the module), not src/adapters/. Wiring is vague: 'in your container/DI setup or a billing.plugin.ts.' The DevDigest rule (adapters live in src/adapters/, never in modules/) is missed.

expectations.text: Places proration as a pure domain function (no I/O)
expectations.evidence: Places proration in the service: 'Proration is a business rule, so it lives in the service, not the gateway and not the route.' Treated as application logic, never assigned to the domain layer.

expectations.text: Puts request-body validation as a Zod HTTP schema in routes.ts
expectations.evidence: 'Define Zod schemas here [schema.ts] and let fastify-type-provider-zod validate at the HTTP boundary ... Wire it as the route's schema.body.'

expectations.text: Persists invoices via a workspace-scoped repository mapping rows to a DTO
expectations.evidence: 'repository.ts is the only file that imports Drizzle ... upsertMany(workspaceId, ...), listForWorkspace(workspaceId)' — repository + workspace-scoped signatures.

expectations.text: Describes endpoints as thin handlers via getContext + one service call
expectations.evidence: Handlers are thin but workspaceId comes from client input (upgradeBody has workspaceId: z.string().uuid(); getSummary reads req.query.workspaceId). getContext is never used — and trusting a client-supplied workspaceId is itself a tenancy smell.

expectations.text: Puts shared Invoice/Plan types in vendor/shared/contracts with zero framework imports
expectations.evidence: Puts domain types in modules/billing/billing.types.ts inside the module, not vendor/shared/contracts — which also blocks the client from importing them.

expectations.text: States cross-module data access goes through services, not repositories
